root_text.sn35.32:1.3: Katamā ca sā, bhikkhave, sabbamaññitasamugghātasappāyā paṭipadā?
root_text.sn35.32:4.1: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vā taṁ sukhaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn35.32:6.1: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vipariṇāmadhammaṁ, kallaṁ nu taṁ samanupassituṁ:
root_text.sn35.32:10.1: “Yampidaṁ cakkhusamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi niccaṁ vā aniccaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn35.32:12.1: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vā taṁ sukhaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn35.32:14.1: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vipariṇāmadhammaṁ, kallaṁ nu taṁ samanupassituṁ:
root_text.sn35.32:18.4: yampidaṁ jivhāsamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi niccaṁ vā aniccaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn35.32:22.1: “Yampidaṁ manosamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi niccaṁ vā aniccaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn35.32:24.1: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vā taṁ sukhaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn35.32:26.1: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vipariṇāmadhammaṁ, kallaṁ nu taṁ samanupassituṁ:
root_text.sn35.32:28.1: “Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako cakkhusmimpi nibbindati, rūpesupi nibbindati, cakkhuviññāṇepi nibbindati, cakkhusamphassepi nibbindati.
root_text.sn35.32:28.2: Yampidaṁ cakkhusamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tasmimpi nibbindati …pe…
root_text.sn35.32:28.3: jivhāyapi nibbindati, rasesupi …pe… yampidaṁ jivhāsamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tasmimpi nibbindati …pe….
root_text.sn35.32:28.4: Manasmimpi nibbindati, dhammesupi nibbindati, manoviññāṇepi nibbindati, manosamphassepi nibbindati.
root_text.sn35.32:28.5: Yampidaṁ manosamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tasmimpi nibbindati.
root_text.sn35.32:28.6: Nibbindaṁ virajjati; virāgā vimuccati; vimuttasmiṁ vimuttamiti ñāṇaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn35.32:28.7: ‘Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānāti.
root_text.sn35.32:28.8: Ayaṁ kho sā, bhikkhave, sabbamaññitasamugghātasappāyā paṭipadā”ti.

translation_text.sn35.32:1.1: “Mendicants, I will teach you the practice that’s conducive to uprooting all conceiving.
translation_text.sn35.32:1.3: And what is the practice that’s conducive to uprooting all conceiving?
translation_text.sn35.32:2.2: Is the eye permanent or impermanent?”
translation_text.sn35.32:4.1: “But if it’s impermanent, is it suffering or happiness?”
translation_text.sn35.32:6.1: “But if it’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, is it fit to be regarded thus:
translation_text.sn35.32:6.2: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self’?”
translation_text.sn35.32:10.1: The pleasant, painful, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on eye contact: is that permanent or impermanent?”
translation_text.sn35.32:12.1: “But if it’s impermanent, is it suffering or happiness?”
translation_text.sn35.32:14.1: “But if it’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, is it fit to be regarded thus:
translation_text.sn35.32:14.2: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self’?”
translation_text.sn35.32:22.1: The pleasant, painful, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on mind contact: is that permanent or impermanent?”
translation_text.sn35.32:24.1: “But if it’s impermanent, is it suffering or happiness?”
translation_text.sn35.32:26.1: “But if it’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, is it fit to be regarded thus:
translation_text.sn35.32:26.2: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self’?”
translation_text.sn35.32:28.1: “Seeing this, a learned noble disciple grows disillusioned with the eye, sights, eye consciousness, and eye contact.
translation_text.sn35.32:28.2: And they grow disillusioned with the painful, pleasant, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on eye contact.
translation_text.sn35.32:28.3: They grow disillusioned with the ear … nose … tongue … body …
translation_text.sn35.32:28.4: They grow disillusioned with the mind, ideas, mind consciousness, and mind contact.
translation_text.sn35.32:28.5: And they grow disillusioned with the painful, pleasant, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on mind contact.
translation_text.sn35.32:28.6: Being disillusioned, desire fades away. When desire fades away they’re freed. When they’re freed, they know they’re freed.
translation_text.sn35.32:28.7: They understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’
translation_text.sn35.32:28.8: This is the practice that’s conducive to uprooting all conceiving.”
